why: cold spec-judge ALIGNED, unchanged since. Edit class ADDITIVE (15 added / 0 modified / 0 removed) against the merge-base, so the freeze self-clears. Carried open, NOT resolved by this gate: check-partition-quality is a control test that cannot fail (control := 0 passes all 18 units) and the role 4.1% figure omits that the engine's own control flags it explainsNothing — both are impl-gate territory.

Round 2 confirmed 5 of 6 round-1 fixes genuinely bound, using mutants the judge authored itself. The sixth — 'two candidate partitions are compared on the same history' — is NOT bound: the round-1 remediation's own new test calls measure() twice on a pre-shared array and never reaches main()'s single readHistory call, which is the wiring the scenario asserts; a mutation to exactly that wiring survived 23/23. The finding's artifact is one the round-1 fix commit 0afaf9b1 itself changed, so rule 4 fires: diverging, HALT, no round 3 attempted. Re-plan is a design call for the owner (add an injection seam to drive main() with two candidates, or extract main()'s candidate mapping into a testable function). partition-quality does not reach implemented; the root spec.md stays approved.

cold impl-judge round 4 IMPLEMENTATION_PASS true, converged. The rule-4 halt (seq 2) was lifted by owner re-plan: main(argv, context = { readHistory }) — a context seam at the CLI boundary (mocking was ruled out by scratch repro: a same-module call cannot be intercepted by mock.module). The last-unbound scenario (two candidate partitions compared on the same history) is now bound at its Then: the test drives the real main() with two candidates and asserts each candidate's JSON measurement equals an independent recomputation over the same commits under the same scope, on a scope-sensitive fixture. Round 3 caught the boundary test asserting the read not the measurement (two survivors); round 4 wrote 5 new mutant classes, zero survivors. All 15 scenarios bound. Root verify 34/34.
